name: Jerry Gray
description: American violinist, arranger, composer, and bandleader (1915-1976)
abstract: Jerry Gray (born Generoso Graziano; July 3, 1915 – August 10, 1976) was an American violinist, arranger, composer, and leader of swing dance orchestras (big bands) bearing his name. He is widely known for his work with jazz and popular music during the Swing era.

about_quotes.speaker: James Chirillo
about_quotes.evidence: So I never met him personally. But he’s the guy that got that band going which was a great band.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Chirillo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Rowles
about_quotes.evidence: I get the call from Jerry Gray to play the Bob Crosby Radio Show... But then, music. Puts a stop to everything. Except there's one time I was on the road with Jerry Gray...

about_quotes.speaker: Artie Shaw
about_quotes.evidence: I trained Jerry Gray, who played lead fiddle in that. I brought him down from Boston. I trained him to work with me... we got to be symbiotic.
about_quotes.doc_title: Artie Shaw — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Jerry Gray only in the context of the interviewer's statement ('he said that he apparently taught someone like Jerry Gray') and the interviewee's own narration of a third party (Artie Shaw) teaching Jerry Gray. The interviewee does not describe a personal relationship with J
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Altman
about_quotes.evidence: And he also convinced Chappel’s, the publishers, to take on Jerry Gray, who they’d never heard of, before all the Glenn Miller stuff, so a very interesting guy.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Altman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 14, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Jerry Gray scored the original 'Begin the Beguine,' and Artie used to give him a chart to go by... Then he had Jerry Gray on his side.
